title: Equivalent fractions (number lines)
description: Practice using number lines to help find equivalent fractions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the number line below to help you complete the equation.**

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac14=\dfrac{p}{12}$

$p=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac14$ is located at the same point on the number line as $\greenD{\dfrac3{12}}$.

$\dfrac14=\greenD{\dfrac3{12}}$

$p=\greenD3$

options.alt: A number line labeled 0 to 1 with tick marks every one-twelfth unit. A point labeled one-fourth appears at the third tick mark after 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the number line below to help you complete the equation.**



[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac{r}{5}=\dfrac8{10}$

$r=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac8{10}$ is located at the same point on the number line as $\greenD{\dfrac45}$.

$\greenD{\dfrac45}=\dfrac8{10}$

$r=\greenD4$

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, an unlabeled tick mark, a tick mark labeled one-fifth, five unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark with a point labeled eight-tenths, one unlabeled tick mark, and an ending tick mark at 1.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the number line below to help you complete the equation.**

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac32=\dfrac{z}{4}$

$z=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac32$ is located at the same point on the number line as $\greenD{\dfrac64}$.

$\dfrac32=\greenD{\dfrac64}$

$z=\greenD6$

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, a tick mark labeled one-fourth, two unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled 1, an unlabeled tick mark, a tick mark with a point labeled three-halves, an unlabeled tick mark, and an ending tick mark at 2.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the number line below to help you complete the equation.**

![An equally spaced number line from 0 to 2, with the point eight tick marks away from 0 labeled 4 thirds and the point 1 tick mark away from 0 labeled 1 sixth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/382c7c96150e73cce5eb38652b6f9faf41991512)

$\dfrac{t}{6}=\dfrac{4}{3}$

$t=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.
![The same number line incremented by sixths and with the point labeled 4 thirds at the same spot as the point labeled 8 sixths.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5c75c63225c9dd7dfbd528eb9975d210238b9e45)

$\dfrac43$ is located at the same point on the number line as $\greenD{\dfrac86}$.

$\greenD{\dfrac86}=\dfrac43$

$t=\greenD8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the number line below to help you complete the equation.**


[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac{x}{8}=\dfrac55$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac55$ is located at the same point on the number line as $\greenD{\dfrac88}$.

$\greenD{\dfrac88}=\dfrac55$

$x=\greenD8$

options.alt: A number line with a starting tick mark at 0, a tick mark labeled one-eighth, six unlabeled tick marks, and a point labeled five-fifths at the ending tick mark 1.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to a fraction equivalent to $\dfrac53$ on each number line.** 

[[☃ number-line 2]]

[[☃ number-line 1]]

**Complete the equation.**

$\dfrac{5}{3} = \dfrac{t}{6}$

$t=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\maroonD{\dfrac53}$.


[[☃ image 1]]
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

$\maroonD{\dfrac{10}6}$ is at the same location as $\maroonD{\dfrac53}$  on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac{10}{6}}=\maroonD{\dfrac53}$

The number lines should be graphed as follows:  


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]



$\dfrac{5}{3} = \dfrac{\maroonD{10}}{6}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to a fraction equivalent to $\dfrac25$ on each number line.** 

[[☃ number-line 2]]

[[☃ number-line 1]]

**Complete the equation.**

$ \dfrac{y}{10}=\dfrac{2}{5}$

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\maroonD{\dfrac25}$.


[[☃ image 1]]
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

$\maroonD{\dfrac4{10}}$ is the at the same location as $\maroonD{\dfrac25}$  on the number line.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac4{10}}=\maroonD{\dfrac25}$

The number lines should be graphed as follows:  


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]



$\dfrac{\maroonD{4}}{10}=\dfrac{2}{5}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to a fraction equivalent to $\dfrac44$ on each number line.** 

[[☃ number-line 2]]

[[☃ number-line 1]]

**Complete the equation.**

$\dfrac{4}{4} = \dfrac{w}{8}$

$w=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\maroonD{\dfrac44}$.


[[☃ image 1]]
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

$\maroonD{\dfrac88}$ is the at the same location as $\maroonD{\dfrac44}$  on the number line.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac88}=\maroonD{\dfrac44}$

The number lines should be graphed as follows:  


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]



$\dfrac{4}{4} = \dfrac{\maroonD{8}}{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to a fraction equivalent to $\dfrac96$ on each number line.** 

[[☃ number-line 2]]

[[☃ number-line 1]]

**Complete the equation.**

$ \dfrac{d}{2}=\dfrac{9}{6}$

$d=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\maroonD{\dfrac96}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

$\maroonD{\dfrac32}$ is the at the same location as $\maroonD{\dfrac96}$  on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\maroonD{\dfrac32}=\maroonD{\dfrac96}$

The number lines should be graphed as follows:  


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]



$\dfrac{\maroonD{3}}{2}=\dfrac{9}{6}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to a fraction equivalent to $\dfrac9{12}$ on each number line.** 


[[☃ number-line 2]]

[[☃ number-line 1]]

**Complete the equation.**

$\dfrac{9}{12} = \dfrac{f}{4}$

$f=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\maroonD{\dfrac9{12}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

$\maroonD{\dfrac34}$ is the at the same location as $\maroonD{\dfrac9{12}}$  on the number line.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\maroonD{\dfrac34}=\maroonD{\dfrac9{12}}$

The number lines should be graphed as follows:  

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]



$\dfrac{9}{12} = \dfrac{\maroonD{3}}{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction is equivalent to point $\blueD{A}$?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
First, let's identify the point on the number line.



[[☃ image 1]]

Point $\blueD{A}$ is located at $\dfrac{6}{8}$.
Numbers are equivalent when they are located at the same point on the number line.

[[☃ image 1]]


$\blueD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ is located at the same point on the number line as $\blueD{\dfrac{6}{8}}$.
Point $\blueD{A}$ is located at $\dfrac{3}{4}$.

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, five unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark at point A, one unlabeled tick mark, and an ending tick mark at 1.

options.alt: A number line that has, moving left to right, a starting tick mark at 0, three unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark at point A, one unlabeled tick mark, and an ending tick mark at 1.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What fraction is equivalent to point $\blueD{A}$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
